script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. Tuesday — here is your MEME recap. The portfolio closed up eleven point four seven percent on the session.

Nvidia just took a nine point three percent stake in Nebius Group. That single disclosure sent Nebius up nearly nineteen percent on Tuesday and pulled the broader artificial intelligence infrastructure trade along with it.

No macro data was available for Tuesday's session. Markets broadly moved in risk-on fashion, with technology and growth names leading the advance. Space, energy infrastructure, and optical networking all caught a bid, suggesting broad sector rotation into high-conviction growth themes rather than a single catalyst driving the tape.

The top story of the session belongs to Nebius Group. Shares surged eighteen point seven eight percent, closing at two hundred sixteen dollars and ninety-two cents — the stock's third consecutive day of gains. The catalyst was confirmation that Nvidia acquired a nine point three percent stake in the company, adding institutional weight to a partnership announced earlier this year. Hedge fund positioning in Nebius more than doubled alongside the news, according to reporting from Yahoo Finance. One note of caution: the company's chairman filed a sale of shares worth one point four million dollars shortly after the disclosure. Insider selling into strength following a nearly three hundred percent one-year return is a detail worth tracking. For this portfolio, Nebius remains a core technology position, and Tuesday's move validates the thesis — but that insider transaction warrants close monitoring going forward.

Applied Optoelectronics was up fifteen point seven six percent on the session — the largest single-day gain among the nine primary holdings. Yahoo Finance reported surging demand for artificial intelligence-driven optical networking products, with the company expanding its United States manufacturing footprint. Analysts flagged intensifying competition as a near-term risk to watch, but Tuesday's price action suggests the market is pricing in the demand story first.

Turning to Redwire Corporation, shares gained nine point five three percent. Two separate catalysts were in play. First, the company opened a new thirty thousand square foot research and microgravity payload development facility in Georgetown, Indiana, focused on pharmaceutical and biotechnology applications. Second, a Seeking Alpha analysis highlighted the company's record backlog, twenty twenty-six revenue targets, and the Andromeda Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract as sources of upside, calling the stock attractive at current valuations near approximately four times sales. Losses remain elevated — something Yahoo Finance noted explicitly — so the backlog narrative needs to translate into margin improvement.

Over at AST SpaceMobile, shares rose ten point three one percent as space stocks broadly staged a risk-on rebound on Tuesday. The company also closed a one point one five billion dollar convertible senior notes offering due twenty thirty-four, raising significant capital for its space-based cellular broadband network buildout. On the cautious side, Jim Cramer publicly warned that AST SpaceMobile is burning cash aggressively and could fall to forty dollars before warranting a closer look. That view sits in direct contrast to a Morgan Stanley report projecting the space economy could reach one trillion dollars in the coming decades, naming AST SpaceMobile among the beneficiaries.

Bloom Energy was up fourteen point eight two percent. JPMorgan raised its price target on the stock from two hundred sixty-seven dollars to three hundred forty-six dollars, maintaining an overweight rating and introducing estimates through fiscal year twenty thirty, projecting four point one gigawatts in product segment deliveries. A separate report confirmed a one point seven billion dollar contract tied to artificial intelligence data center demand. That combination of institutional analyst support and a major commercial contract drove significant volume on Tuesday.

Meanwhile, Lumentum Holdings gained nine point four one percent on Tuesday. No company-specific news drove the move — the gain appears tied to broader optical networking and photonics sector strength, consistent with the Applied Optoelectronics move. Lumentum's upcoming earnings guidance will be critical for confirming whether demand trends are accelerating.

TeraWulf was up five point three six percent on the session. The move was the most modest among the primary holdings, but still positive. Bitcoin mining infrastructure names broadly followed cryptocurrency market direction on Tuesday, and TeraWulf's high-performance computing pivot continues to be the longer-term catalyst to monitor.

IREN Limited gained two point seven one percent — the smallest advance in the portfolio on Tuesday. Like TeraWulf, IREN operates in the digital infrastructure and bitcoin mining space, and the session's gain reflects sector tailwinds rather than company-specific news. Watch for any updates on power capacity expansion or high-performance computing contract announcements.

Sandisk Corporation closed up fourteen point two seven percent on Tuesday. No specific news was attributed to the move. Flash storage demand trends and any commentary from adjacent semiconductor names remain the key signals to track heading into the back half of the week.

Three data points to watch in the next forty-eight hours. First, any Federal Reserve speaker commentary on rate trajectory, which would directly affect growth stock valuations across this portfolio. Second, further institutional disclosures related to Nebius Group, including any additional Nvidia filing details. Third, watch for any AST SpaceMobile operational updates following the completion of its convertible note offering — how that capital is deployed will matter.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch closely for any Bloom Energy contract execution updates and whether Nebius Group can sustain momentum into a fourth consecutive session.
